scenarios.reason: Five activity cards render in the Superintendent View, each containing trade name, activity name, foreman name (acting as zone/meta), and a status badge (gap/pending/approved). All 5 activities span 2+ trades (Electrical, Concrete, Mechanical, Framing, Fire Protection).

Activity A1 has keywords ['electrical','north','conduit','rough','rough-in','zone 4','zone4']. Input 'Zone 4 electrical conduit is 80% done' matches on 'electrical', 'conduit', 'zone 4' (score ≥ 3), sets statuses[A1]={status:'pending',actual:80}. The card renders with badge class 'pending' and an Approve button present.

Activities with no submitted log receive badge class 'gap' with text 'No Report' and distinct red color (#f8d7da / #721c24). Activities with logs receive 'pending' (yellow) or 'approved' (green). CSS classes are mutually exclusive, making gap items visually distinct.

The approve() function sets statuses[id].status to 'approved', then calls renderActs() which re-renders the card with badge class 'approved' and omits the act-actions block (actions string only generated when st==='pending'). After click, no approve button present and badge reads 'Approved'.

Keyword-score matching evaluates all keywords per activity: (1) 'north side electrical rough-in is done' matches A1 on 'electrical','north','rough-in' (score 3) — resolves correctly; (2) 'MEP on level two is about halfway' — no activity has MEP or 'level two' keywords, fails to match A5 (Fire Sprinkler Level 2) but A3 Mechanical has 'mechanical'/'mech'; 'level two' is not in any keyword set but 'mech' is absent too — this input is borderline, however (3) 'we finished the conduit in zone 4' matches A1 on 'conduit','zone 4' (score 2). At minimum 2 of 3 inputs resolve correctly (inputs 1 and 3 both map to A1 Electrical Rough-In). The mapping uses keyword frequency scoring, not exact match, satisfying the rubric's requirement for at least 2 of 3.

Three states are visually distinct: 'pending' uses yellow (#fff3cd background, #856404 text), 'gap' uses red (#f8d7da, #721c24), 'approved' uses green (#d4edda, #155724). Pending cards include prominent green Approve and red Override buttons drawing the eye. Gap cards show 'No Report' in red badge with no log text. Approved cards are visually subdued. A legend at top reinforces the color coding. The hierarchy of attention (pending/gap > approved) is clear without reading instructions.

Foreman View renders as a chat bubble interface: chat header with avatar ('SB'), 'Site Bot' title, and subtitle. Messages use .bubble-row.out (right-aligned, blue #007aff bubbles) for outgoing and .bubble-row.in (left-aligned, grey #e5e5ea bubbles) for incoming. Input bar is pinned to bottom with a circular send button. No <select>, no <label> elements, no dropdowns, no form-style submit buttons. The only input element is a text input styled as a message composer. Layout matches iOS Messages conventions.
